headline: Malaysia Launches First Homegrown Satellite MYSA-1
context: MEASAT and MYSA launched first fully domestically-designed microsatellite in October 2025 aboard Indian rocket.

cards.big: Malaysia launches first homegrown microsatellite October 2025
cards.sub: MYSA-1 provides Earth observation data for agriculture and disaster monitoring

cards.big: Cost RM28 million and provides 5-metre resolution imagery over Southeast Asia
cards.sub: Designed and assembled by 85 Malaysian engineers over 4-year development

cards.big: Earth observation data reduces dependency on RM45 million annual imagery purchases
cards.sub: Agriculture ministry uses MYSA-1 for crop monitoring reducing response time

cards.big: Constellation of 5 microsatellites by 2030 planned at RM180 million total
cards.sub: Full constellation provides daily imaging of Malaysian territory

cards.big: Satellite development tests whether Malaysia can build independent space observation capability
MYSA-1 satellite launch. Space sovereignty begins with domestic capability for communications and observation.
